summary: Captured from site evidence on www.booking.com showed the clearest trust risk in checkout flow. The strongest observed signal was "4 scenario interactions were captured: "Selected offer "Noelle Nashville, United States 8.4 8.4 Very" -> "Opened hotel detail "Drury Plaza Hotel Nashville Downt", and persona comparisons still showed materially different friction across the run.

persona_comparison.headline: Privacy-first users followed a more cautious review path before committing. Example captured evidence: "3 scenario interactions were captured: "Selected offer "Las Vegas"" -> "Opened hotel detail "Hampton Inn Las Vegas Strip".

Price-conscious users followed the most offer-driven checkout path. Example captured evidence: "3 scenario interactions were captured: "Selected offer "Knights Inn Chattanooga Chattanooga, United " -> "Opened hotel d".

Exit-intent behavior triggered the deepest review and policy path. Example captured evidence: "4 scenario interactions were captured: "Selected offer "Noelle Nashville, United States 8.4 8.4 Very" -> "Opened hotel d".

scenario_breakdown.headline: The checkout journey required additional offer, detail, or reserve steps after intent was established. Example captured evidence: "3 scenario interactions were captured: "Selected offer "Las Vegas"" -> "Opened hotel detail "Hampton Inn Las Vegas Strip".
